name: Pre-GPT Short-Answer Scoring Augmentation on SciEntsBank
statement: Lun and colleagues provide an older, non-LLM example where augmented short-answer data were used to supplement limited ASAS training data, using back-translation, correct-answer reference augmentation, and column-swapping variants on SciEntsBank.
population_or_context: Science automatic short-answer scoring on SemEval-2013/SciEntsBank.
quantitative_note: The strongest combined swap variant improved UA macro-F1 over the original data baseline by about 2, 2, and 1 points for two-, three-, and five-way classification, respectively.
evaluation_method: BERTBASE scorer performance compared with accuracy, macro-F1, and weighted-F1 across original and augmented training conditions.
sparsity_or_imbalance_motivation: The study frames augmentation as a response to ASAS training-data scarcity and the cost of collecting more labeled student answers.
synthetic_data_research_role: Training-data supplementation and scorer-performance improvement, not direct scorer validation.
reported_metrics: Table 3 reports A-origin vs B4-swap-A-B1 UA Acc/M-F1/W-F1 as 0.8055/0.8003/0.8047 vs 0.8277/0.8225/0.8267 for two-way classification, 0.7481/0.6795/0.7435 vs 0.7631/0.6934/0.7578 for three-way classification, and 0.6875/0.5115/0.6857 vs 0.7002/0.5256/0.6998 for five-way classification.
limitations: The main synthesis-ready result is for the unseen-answer condition.
Back-translated examples inherited labels rather than receiving independent human scoring.
source_locator: Full-text PDF; Abstract; Multiple Data Augmentation Strategies; Experiments/Dataset; Tables 2-4; Conclusion and Future Work.
